metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Fear of academic failure and disappointing parents.
Perfectionism; cannot start without the "perfect" opening line.
Identity crisis; feels essay doesn't represent "true self".
Physical over-arousal; body not tired despite late hour.
Rumination loop; feels sleep equals "giving up" on solving the conflict.

metadata.student_feeling: FOMO: team is counting on me.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Strong sense of duty and loyalty to online teammates.

metadata.student_feeling: Compelled: need closure on the plot.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Narrative obsession; inability to tolerate delayed gratification.
Catastrophic thinking; believes a poor performance will define him.
Guilt associated with "unproductive" leisure or sleep.
Hyper-vigilance; safety feels dependent on remaining alert.
Creative block coupled with high stakes evaluation.

metadata.student_feeling: In the 'flow' state: losing track of time.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Creative immersion; stopping feels like breaking a magical state.
Physiological arousal; body still in "competition mode".

metadata.student_feeling: Pressure to lead and set an example.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Responsibility overload; feels like failing the team if unprepared.
Social anxiety; rehearsing conversations and worrying about fitting in.
Avoidance; staying up feels like prolonging freedom before an unpleasant obligation.
Problem-solving urgency; believes breakthrough is imminent with more time.

metadata.student_feeling: Feeling the urgency of the cause.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Moral imperative; personal needs feel trivial compared to a global cause.

metadata.situation_faced: In the middle of writing a climax
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Narrative autonomy; feels like a vessel for characters who demand their story be told.

metadata.student_feeling: Shame and need to 'fix' it immediately.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Compensatory behavior; believes self-punishment (loss of sleep) is required for poor performance.
Future anxiety; fixating on a symbol of adulthood and freedom.

metadata.student_feeling: Nothing enjoyable to do, so why sleep?
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Anhedonia; sleep is just another form of boring inactivity.

metadata.student_feeling: Frustrated and mentally exhausted but not sleepy.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Mental fatigue vs. sleepiness; brain is tired from effort but not ready for sleep.
Existential need; feels this private time is crucial for self-discovery.

metadata.student_feeling: Fear of consequences if not met.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: External pressure internalized; the "consequence" (parental disapproval) feels more immediate than sleep deprivation.

metadata.student_feeling: Anxiety about the anxiety of not sleeping.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Psychophysiological insomnia; fear of sleeplessness itself perpetuates it.
Phobia; requires a specific coping ritual light/music that delays sleep.

metadata.student_feeling: Night feels like the only 'quiet' time.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Circadian shift; depression can cause phase delay and make nights feel more "alive".

metadata.student_feeling: Feels like a waste of time, resists it.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Intellectual rebellion; engaging in more stimulating activities at night as compensation.

metadata.student_feeling: Stress and need to monitor the situation.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Hyper-vigilance in family conflict; feels responsible as an emotional barometer.

metadata.student_feeling: Frustrated by lack of control over environment.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Environmental frustration; cannot sleep until external stimulus stops.
Positive future fantasy; a pleasurable escape from current stressors.

metadata.student_feeling: Can't get images out of mind.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Mental intrusion; fearful imagery hijacks attention.
Real-world precarity; abstract academic stress replaced by concrete survival anxiety.

metadata.situation_faced: Scrolling through peers' fun social posts
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Social comparison; painful reminder of perceived social failure.
Rebellion against lack of autonomy; reclaiming time as an act of self-assertion.
Body image obsession; mirror or social media triggers a negative thought spiral.

metadata.student_feeling: Just installed it, need to try it NOW.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Novelty seeking; inability to defer gratification due to underdeveloped prefrontal cortex.

metadata.situation_faced: Struggling with assignment in non-native language
metadata.student_feeling: Takes twice as long, feels hopeless.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Cognitive overload and frustration; task is inherently more exhausting.

metadata.situation_faced: Replaying an awkward interaction from today
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Rumination on social faux pas; intense emotional memory.
Transition anxiety; mental preparation for change of rules/environment.

metadata.student_feeling: Hoping for a new post or sign.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Dopamine-seeking behavior; intermittent reinforcement from checking feeds.
Sensory aversion; tactile discomfort prevents relaxation.

metadata.student_feeling: 'In the zone' after hours of avoidance.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Delayed activation; panic-induced focus is powerful and feels too valuable to stop.

metadata.student_feeling: Can't be seen to work less hard.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Sibling rivalry; sleep is perceived as "losing" a competition of diligence.

metadata.student_feeling: Angry and wanting to assert independence.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Autonomy struggle; controlling sleep is a domain of personal control.
Traumatic sleep association; fear of a specific sleep phenomenon.

metadata.situation_faced: Pain is worse when lying down
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Physical pain; sleep posture may exacerbate discomfort.

metadata.student_feeling: Trying to secretly cram to catch up.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Shame and secrecy; double burden of learning and concealment.
Pharmacological; stimulant blocking adenosine effects.

metadata.student_feeling: Sleeping feels like surrendering to the sadness.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Grief avoidance; staying awake avoids the quiet moments where loss is felt most acutely.

metadata.student_feeling: If nothing matters, why follow rules?
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Existential nihilism; undermines the rationale for any long-term beneficial habit.

metadata.situation_faced: Teacher implied they could do better
metadata.student_feeling: Don't want to disappoint authority figure.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: External validation; highly sensitive to perceived expectations.

metadata.situation_faced: College acceptance but aid forms are complex
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Realistic future threat; abstract academic pressure replaced by concrete administrative/financial hurdle.

metadata.situation_faced: Heard a nasty rumor about self
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Reputational threat; social survival feels at stake.
Somatic anxiety; performance anxiety manifests physically.

metadata.situation_faced: Lost sense of how late it is
metadata.student_feeling: Doesn't feel like 11 PM at all.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Blue light & content immersion; disrupts melatonin and time perception.

metadata.situation_faced: Part of a community parents disapprove of
metadata.student_feeling: Only safe access time is late night.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Closeted identity; this community is a core part of social support.

metadata.situation_faced: Pressure to grow up too fast
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Developational resistance; using night to engage in age-inappropriate play.
Environmental resignation; has given up on the possibility of good sleep.
Trauma response; hyper-arousal and fear of re-experiencing.

metadata.situation_faced: Found a fascinating wikipedia rabbit hole
metadata.student_feeling: Just learning for fun, not for school.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Intrinsic curiosity; learning is self-directed and highly rewarding.
Existential anxiety; big questions feel more pressing in the quiet of night.

metadata.student_feeling: The narrative world is more appealing.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Preference for fictional reality; more engaging and satisfying than real life.
Autonomy testing; the conflict itself is the point.
Ruminative anger; planning provides a sense of control and justice.

metadata.situation_faced: Creating a new playlist or discovering music
metadata.student_feeling: In a state of musical euphoria.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Aesthetic immersion; emotional journey through music is compelling.

metadata.situation_faced: Parents are out for the evening
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Attachment anxiety; safety is tied to primary caregivers' presence.

metadata.situation_faced: Intended to sleep but got sidetracked
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Attentional drift; poor executive function on initiating the sleep sequence.

metadata.student_feeling: Cannot be the one who fails.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Family legacy; pressure is multigenerational and tied to identity.
Oneirophobia; fear of the unconscious content of one's own mind.

metadata.student_feeling: Night is the only controllable time.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Compensation for lack of agency; controlling sleep schedule is a proxy for controlling life.

metadata.student_feeling: Fixated on solving this visible flaw.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Appearance-based shame; high emotional salience of the issue.

metadata.student_feeling: Fear of not knowing something important.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Compulsive checking; driven by anxiety and the intermittent reward of "important" info.

metadata.situation_faced: Had a long nap or slept in
metadata.student_feeling: Circadian rhythm is simply out of sync.
metadata.primary_annoyance_reason: Biological mismatch; sleep drive is low at the socially mandated bedtime.
